content: Ralph Loop: "Make a chess game with AI opponent"

Vision: The screenshot displays a functional, two-color chessboard with chess pieces strategically placed for play. Everything related to the board setup—the squares and the distinct piece models—appears rendered correctly and ready for gameplay. However, the composition feels incomplete due to the vast, un

My assessment is that the initial board setup works well, but the overall game interface is incomplete, leaving a large unused space that needs utility for status indicators or move logging. I will refactor the UI layout to incorporate dedicated areas for game status just below the chessboard area within the canvas.

I will update the structure to utilize the full 700px height by adjusting the drawing logic to reserve space at the bottom for status displays, making the game feel more contained a

Vision: This screenshot displays a standard 8x8 chessboard populated with all the pieces for both White and Black, set up for the start of a game. The rendering is clean and functional, showing the pieces in their initial algebraic positions against a neutral background. No elements appear broken or missing

The board setup is clean and the basic input handling for mouse and touch is connected. The biggest issue remaining is the lack of any tangible visual feedback when a piece is selected or when a move is attempted, making the interaction feel unresponsive.

I will implement visual selection highlighting on the board when a piece is clicked, and I will update the interaction handler to manage the 'selected' state with better visual feedback.
